trade.summary: Polonia-Stati Uniti trade is substantial and growing. Polonia exports machinery, automotive components, electronics, furniture, chemicals, and food to the Stati Uniti. The Stati Uniti exports aircraft, military equipment, LNG, software, and pharmaceuticals to Polonia. The Stati Uniti is one of Polonia's most important trading partners outside the EU. Stati Uniti LNG exports to Polonia have grown significantly as part of Polonia's energy diversification strategy.

diplomatic.status: strategic partnership — one of the closest US bilateral relationships in Europe
diplomatic.summary: The Stati Uniti-Polonia relationship is one of the closest bilateral partnerships in NATO Europe. Polonia is considered the most reliable Stati Uniti ally on the continent by many Stati Uniti policymakers, given its consistent support for strong Atlantic solidarity, high defense spending, and unequivocal backing of Ucraina. Polonia has hosted Stati Uniti presidents, signed bilateral defense cooperation agreements, and positions itself as America's indispensable partner in Central-Eastern Europe. Some friction occurred during Trump's first term over burden-sharing rhetoric, but the underlying alliance is deep. Polonia has consistently supported Stati Uniti positions on Russia, Cina, and Middle East.
recent_incidents: Ongoing discussions on permanent Stati Uniti military basing in Polonia (Fort Trump concept)
Stati Uniti-Polonia nuclear energy cooperation agreements signed
Polonia publicly critical of any Stati Uniti engagement with Russia without Ukrainian involvement

military.summary: The Stati Uniti-Polonia military relationship is the most significant bilateral defence relationship in Polonia's history. The Stati Uniti has ~10,000+ troops permanently stationed or rotated through Polonia, including armored brigade combat teams, air force elements, and intelligence assets. Polonia has purchased F-35s, HIMARS, Abrams tanks, Patriot air defense, and other Stati Uniti systems worth $30+ billion. Permanent Stati Uniti basing discussions are ongoing. Polonia hosts V Corps (Stati Uniti Army) forward headquarters. Joint exercises are extensive and frequent. Polonia has more Stati Uniti forward-deployed capability than any other NATO ally.
military.alliance_status: NATO allies — premier bilateral defence partnership in NATO Europe

financial.summary: Stati Uniti companies are major investors in Polonia. Google, Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Intel have major data center and R&D investments in Polonia. Stati Uniti private equity has invested heavily in Polish logistics, real estate, and technology. Polish companies increasingly list on Stati Uniti markets and seek Stati Uniti venture capital. The Stati Uniti-Polonia Investment Fund has supported bilateral commercial engagement.

energy.summary: Energy is a growing pillar of the relationship. Polonia was an early purchaser of Stati Uniti LNG (Polskie LNG terminal in Swinoujscie), and Stati Uniti LNG is now a major component of Polonia's gas supply. Stati Uniti companies (Westinghouse) are contracted to build Polonia's first nuclear power plants. Stati Uniti energy equipment and services companies are active in Polish power sector modernization.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific partnership across engineering, IT, life sciences, and social sciences. Polish universities have deep partnerships with Stati Uniti research institutions (MIT, Stanford, University of California system). Polish-American diaspora (Polish-Americans number ~10 million) maintains cultural and scientific bridges. NSF and Polish NCN run joint research programs. The Stati Uniti is the top destination for Polish researchers abroad.
